title: Train-Test Density-Ratio Reweighting For Covariate Shift
summary: The dataset already shows distribution-shift risk: unseen drivers/entities in test and possible 2025/session mix changes.
rationale: The dataset already shows distribution-shift risk: unseen drivers/entities in test and possible 2025/session mix changes. Importance-weighting literature says when train and test covariates differ, reweighting training rows toward the test distribution can improve generalization without using labels from test.
implementation_hint: Train a lightweight domain classifier to distinguish train vs test rows using raw/event features; convert its outputs to clipped density-ratio weights and refit the main model or only the final blender with those weights. Evaluate with both ordinary grouped CV and a train-vs-test AUC diagnostic.
expected_effect: Better leaderboard robustness on underrepresented 2025/rare-entity regimes and a useful hedge against overfitting to older or easier races.
risk: Estimated weights can be unstable and over-amplify outliers; clipping and a strong ablation against unweighted folds are necessary.
